title: Marinated Indian Pancetta Curry
description: A spicy, juicy, light Indian dish featuring pancetta cooked in a clay pot, perfect for lacto-vegetarian friendly meals.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a clay pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add pancetta slices and cook until they start to crisp, about 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Toss in sliced onion and minced garlic, sauté until onion turns translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in diced tomatoes, cumin, coriander, red chili powder, and salt, cooking until tomatoes soften, about 7 minutes.
Reduce heat to low, add yogurt, garam masala, and black pepper, stirring until the mixture coats the pancetta and tomato mixture, about 5 minutes.
Cover the pot with its lid, let the curry simmer gently for 15 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Stir once and ladle the curry into serving bowls, garnish with fresh cilantro.
Serve hot over steamed rice or flatbread.
